top_liked.label: #7 @kristenzisek - The stock m
top_liked.text: How do we prepare to capitalize on earnings this week? Any practical tips?

top_liked.label: #7 @kristenzisek - The stock m
top_liked.text: Depends on what your goal is! First — don’t chase. If a stock is already up big going into earnings, expectations are high, and that’s risky IMO. Second — know what’s priced in. The options market is literally telling you the expected move. If the stock doesn’t beat that, you can still lose money… even if the company does well. Look at the implied earnings moves. Third — think about how much upside is actually left. A lot of these big names — like Alphabet or Amazon — some of them are already near all-time highs or up a lot in the past six months. So you have to ask… how much higher can they really go short-term? Maybe a lot, who knows? But something I’m thinking about. Fourth — separate short-term trades from long-term investing. I might love a company long-term — like I own Alphabet — but that doesn’t mean I’m adding right before earnings. Those are two different decisions. And finally — if you’re going to play earnings, I personally treat that as risk capital only. Money you can afford to lose, and money you don’t need for years. Cause anything can happen! If I’m taking a big swing during earnings (AKA doing something stupid! Which I do from time to time 😊), I’d generally rather do it in something with real upside potential — not necessarily a mega-cap that might move 3–5%. Again, only using money I’m comfortable losing! Earnings can be an opportunity — but they’re not a strategy.
